# npm-exists

Check if an npm package name is taken. Zero dependencies, uses native fetch.

## Summary

`npm-exists` is a minimal Node.js library and CLI tool that queries the npm registry (or any npm-compatible registry) to check whether a package name is already registered. It returns the full registry metadata object on success, or `false` for a 404.

## Installation

npm install npm-exists

## Core API

```js
import npmExists, { getNpmUrl } from 'npm-exists'

// Returns metadata object or false
const result = await npmExists('react')

// Returns registry URL string
const url = getNpmUrl('react') // https://registry.npmjs.org/react

// Custom registry
const result = await npmExists('my-pkg', 'https://my.private.registry.io')
```

## CLI

```
npm-exists <package-name> [registry-url]
```

Exit 0 = exists, Exit 1 = not found.

## Requirements

Node >= 18 (native fetch). Zero runtime dependencies.

## Source

https://github.com/jayverstraete/npm-exists
